summary: Shan Fu (Xu Shu); deploys his first formation against Cao Ren at Xinye/Fancheng — breaks the Eight Gates of the Mysterious Yang. Then receives the forged letter from his mother and leaves Liu Bei in tears, but not before recommending Zhuge Liang at Sleeping Dragon Ridge.
Xu Shu; same character as Shan Fu (his original name); the chapter's defining moment is his parting recommendation of Zhuge Liang to Liu Bei: 'a sleeping dragon among men'.
Cao Ren; defeated at Xinye by Shan Fu's breaking of his Eight Gates formation; loses Fancheng to Zhao Yun's surprise attack.
Li Dian; with Cao Ren at Xinye/Fancheng.

Forges the letter that lures Shan Fu away from Liu Bei — Cheng Yu's scheme. Loses Cao Ren's army at Xinye but gains Xu Shu's nominal allegiance, even if Xu Shu vows never to draw up a single plan for him.
Cheng Yu; designs the forged-letter scheme — impersonates Xu Shu's mother's hand to lure Xu Shu to Xuchang.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Liu Bei; wins his first major engagement under Shan Fu's strategy at Xinye; loses Shan Fu to the forged letter; sets out toward Sleeping Dragon Ridge after Xu Shu's recommendation.
Guan Yu; commands part of the Xinye action.
Zhang Fei; commands part of the Xinye action.
Zhao Yun; takes Fancheng with the surprise attack while Cao Ren is unbalanced at Xinye.
Liu Feng's debut — adopted by Liu Bei in this chapter as his nominal son (before Liu Shan's birth was confirmed as the heir).
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei at Xinye.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Liu Biao; named in passing — Liu Bei's nominal lord.
Sima Hui; named in passing in the chapter's recap as the recluse who started the Sleeping Dragon recommendation.
Pang Tong; named in passing — the second 'Young Phoenix' name on Sima Hui's list.
Zhang Liang the Han strategist; cited in a rhetorical aside about Liu Bei wanting his own.
Zhang Liang (duplicate record).
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Wang Xun; minor figure named in passing.
Zhuge Gui; Zhuge Liang's father; named only in his lineage.
Zhuge Jun's debut — Zhuge Liang's younger brother; appears in the chapter's setup at Sleeping Dragon Ridge.
Zhuge Liang; not yet appeared in person — his name introduced at the chapter's end via Xu Shu's recommendation. The setup for chapter 37's three-visits arc.
Zhuge Xuan; Zhuge Liang's late uncle; named in the lineage.

Liu Bei; the famous Three Visits to Sleeping Dragon Ridge begin. First visit: Zhuge Liang isn't home — encounters Zhuge Jun. Second visit through deep snow: still not home; meets Zhuge Liang's father-in-law Huang Chengyan and brother Zhuge Jun, and learns to wait patiently. The arc spans the chapter.

Zhuge Liang; not directly met yet. The chapter sketches him through his friends and family — Cui Zhouping, Shi Guangyuan, Meng Jian — and through his own brother and father-in-law. Each conversation deepens the portrait without revealing the man.
Zhuge Jun; meets Liu Bei twice and tells him his brother is again away on a wandering trip.
Zhuge Jin; Zhuge Liang's elder brother; named in passing — already in Sun Quan's service.

Xu Shu; arrives at Cao Cao's court; his mother berates him for abandoning Liu Bei for the false letter and hangs herself rather than have her name used to corrupt him. Xu Shu vows lifelong silence at Cao Cao's councils.
Guan Yu; impatient through the first two visits — argues Liu Bei is showing 'too much deference'.
Zhang Fei; openly angry through the first two visits; threatens to bring Zhuge Liang back tied with a rope.
Receives Xu Shu in Xuchang after his mother's suicide; Xu Shu sits silently at every council from this chapter onward.
Sima Hui; reappears in the chapter to confirm Zhuge Liang's quality — 'his talents shall illumine the empire' — but laments 'though he found his lord, alas not his moment'.
Chen Yu; minor figure named in passing.
Cui Jun; a Cui Zhouping form; one of Zhuge Liang's circle whom Liu Bei meets en route to the ridge.
Meng Jian (Meng Gongwei); one of Zhuge Liang's scholar friends whom Liu Bei meets in the second visit's tavern scene.
Shi Tao (Shi Guangyuan); another of Zhuge Liang's circle whom Liu Bei meets at the tavern.
Liu Fu; senior court official at Xuchang; presents Xu Shu's case to Cao Cao.
Sun Quan; named in passing in the regional roll.
Cheng Yu; with Cao Cao at Xuchang.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu; running Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Zhu Jun; named in passing in the Xuchang court.
Zhang Yan; named in passing.
Yin Kui; named in passing.
Yin Kui (duplicate record).
Zhang Liang the Han strategist; cited in Liu Bei's longing rhetoric — 'where is my Zhang Liang?'.
Zhang Liang (duplicate record).
Liu Xiu; opening-frame reference to the Latter Han founder.

Zhuge Liang's introduction in person — the famous Third Visit. Found napping in his thatched cottage at the foot of the ridge. After Liu Bei waits respectfully for hours, Zhuge Liang wakes, exchanges the formal greetings, and lays out the Longzhong Plan: hold Jingzhou and Yizhou, ally with Sun Quan, restore the Han. Joins Liu Bei at twenty-seven.

Third Visit chapter — fasts for three days, walks the last mile on foot in winter to the ridge, stands respectfully outside the cottage while Zhuge Liang naps. Receives the Longzhong Plan. 'I am as a fish that has found water,' he says when Zhuge Liang agrees to follow him out.
Zhuge Jun; Zhuge Liang's brother; runs the family household when Zhuge Liang goes off to follow Liu Bei.
Guan Yu; with Liu Bei at the Third Visit.
Zhang Fei; with Liu Bei at the Third Visit; muttering through the long wait.
Xu Shu; named in passing.
Off-stage at Xuchang as Zhuge Liang joins Liu Bei — the strategic shift the chapter sets up will define the next two books.
Liu Biao; named in passing as Liu Bei's nominal lord.
Sima Hui; named in passing — the prophecy-confirmer.

Sun Quan; the Wu storyline picks up in the second half — at Lady Wu's deathbed receives the famous final injunction: 'consult Zhang Zhao for internal matters and Zhou Yu for external matters,' and is told to revere Lu Su as a teacher. Begins the Huang Zu campaign against Liu Biao for his father's revenge.
Lady Wu; Sun Quan and Sun Ce's mother; dies in this chapter giving Sun Quan the final injunction about Zhang Zhao and Zhou Yu.
Sun Ce; named in passing — his mother's dying instructions are honoured.
Sun Yi; Sun Quan's brother; killed by his own officer Bian Hong at Danyang — the chapter's sub-plot scandal.
Sun Yi (duplicate record).
Sun Yi (duplicate record).
Bian Hong; murders Sun Yi at Danyang; executed by Lady Xu's vengeance plot.
Lady Xu; Sun Yi's widow; engineers Bian Hong's execution to avenge her husband — the chapter's standout female scene.
Lady Xu (duplicate record).
Gui Lan; co-conspirator in Sun Yi's murder; killed in Lady Xu's revenge.
Dai Yuan; co-conspirator; killed in Lady Xu's revenge.
Sun Gao; named in passing.
Huang Zu; Sun Quan's prime target — defending Xiakou against the southern campaign.

Gan Ning's debut — 'Silk-sailed Pirate' originally under Huang Zu; defects to Sun Quan after Huang Zu treats him as a brigand. Kills Deng Long during the Xiakou crossing — the engagement that finally breaks Huang Zu's defence.
Deng Long; Huang Zu's officer; killed by Gan Ning in the river crossing.
Chen Jiu; Huang Zu's officer; killed by Lu Meng in the river crossing.
Su Fei; Huang Zu's commander; captured by Sun Quan and reserved for execution alongside Huang Zu.
Zhou Yu; commands the southern campaign against Huang Zu / Liu Biao.
Ling Cao; killed at Xiakou by Gan Ning — the death that creates the Ling Tong / Gan Ning feud for the next decade.
Lu Ji; minor figure named in passing.
Lu Ji (duplicate record).
Lu Xun; named in passing — will rise to prominence later.
Luo Tong; one of Sun Quan's officers.
Pan Zhang; named in passing — future captor of Guan Yu in chapter 76.
Xu Sheng; named in passing among Wu officers.
Ding Feng; named in passing among Wu officers.
Ding Feng (duplicate record).
Dong Xi; named in passing among Wu officers.
Zhang Zhao; Sun Quan's senior civil officer.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong; civil officer.
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Gu Yong; civil officer at Sun Quan's court.
Zhu Huan; minor figure named in passing.
Wu Jing; Sun family relative named in passing.
Wu Can; minor figure named in passing.
Xue Yong; minor figure named in passing.
Yan Jun; minor figure named in passing.
Yan Jun (duplicate record).
Yan Jun (duplicate record).
Kan Ze; named in passing — will play a role in chapter 47.
Cheng Bing; minor figure named in passing.
Fu Ying; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Wen; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Wen (duplicate record).
Liu Zhang; named in passing in the regional roll.
Dong Zhuo; named in passing in the chapter's recap.
Yuan Shao; named in passing — long dead.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Liu Xiu; opening-frame reference to the Latter Han founder.

Zhuge Liang's first command — designs the Bowang Slope fire ambush against Xiahou Dun's 100,000-strong invasion. Guan Yu, Zhang Fei and Zhao Yun execute it; the Wei van is annihilated. Zhang Fei admits 'Zhuge Liang is really a fine strategist' — the chapter that wins his brothers' trust.
Liu Bei; deploys Zhuge Liang's sword and seal to the brothers as authority; watches the Bowang Slope fire ambush succeed and consolidates the army's loyalty under his new strategist.
Xiahou Dun; commands Cao Cao's first probing invasion of Xinye with 100,000 troops; led into the fire trap at Bowang Slope and crushed. Returns to Cao Cao bound and asking for execution.
Li Dian; warns Xiahou Dun against the narrow road at Bowang Slope; ignored, then proven right.
Yu Jin; warns Xiahou Dun alongside Li Dian; ignored.
Han Hao; Cao Cao officer at Bowang Slope.
Xiahou Lan; Xiahou Dun's cousin; killed at Bowang Slope.
Guan Yu; executes part of the Bowang Slope ambush.
Zhang Fei; executes part of the Bowang Slope ambush — admits Zhuge Liang's brilliance at chapter's end.
Zhao Yun; executes part of the Bowang Slope ambush.
Guan Ping; with Guan Yu at Bowang Slope.
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei at Xinye.
Cao Cao; receives Xiahou Dun's bound return after Bowang Slope; sets in motion the great southern campaign of 500,000 troops with Sima Yi advising for the first time.
Sima Yi's debut — recommended to Cao Cao after Bowang Slope; introduced as 'a wolf's gaze' figure. Cao Cao recognises the danger but engages him anyway.
Sima Fang; Sima Yi's father; named in his lineage.
Sima Lang; Sima Yi's brother; named in his lineage.

Liu Qi; thrice begs Zhuge Liang for counsel on his own survival under the stepmother Lady Cai; receives the famous Spring-and-Autumn parallel to Shen Sheng vs Chong'er. Goes to Jiangxia under Zhuge Liang's advice.
Liu Biao; ailing; about to die. Tries to summon Liu Bei to the deathbed.
Cai Mao; blocks Liu Biao from summoning Liu Bei; positions the Cai faction for the imminent succession.
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei at Xinye.
Mi Fang; with Liu Bei at Xinye.
Mao Jie; named in passing at Xuchang.
Xun Yu; running Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xu Shu; named in passing — silent at Cao Cao's councils.
Cui Yan; named in passing at Xuchang.
Gan Ning; with Sun Quan in the south.
Cheng Pu; with Sun Quan.
Huang Zu; finally captured at Xiakou by Lu Meng; beheaded and his head offered to Sun Jian's grave — Sun Quan's father is avenged at last.
Sun Quan; finally kills Huang Zu, avenging his father Sun Jian after 14 years. Then receives Lu Su's plan for the post-Liu Biao Jingzhou.
Ling Cao; named in passing — killed by Gan Ning at Xiakou.
Sun Kuang; with Sun Quan in the south.
Su Fei; executed alongside Huang Zu when Sun Quan returns to the south.
Zhou Yu; with Sun Quan in the southern victory.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan in the south.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Biao; dies in this chapter, leaving Jingzhou to Liu Cong (not Liu Qi) under Lady Cai and Cai Mao's manipulation. The handover happens before Liu Bei or Liu Qi can intervene.
Lady Cai; engineers Liu Biao's deathbed swap of the heir from Liu Qi to her son Liu Cong; surrenders Jingzhou to Cao Cao within months — then is murdered by Cao Cao's order on the road.
Cai Mao; engineers Liu Cong's succession and Jingzhou's surrender to Cao Cao. Cao Cao appoints him admiral of the Cao navy — the post that will set up Jiang Gan's scheme at Red Cliffs.

Liu Xin (Liu Cong); 14 years old; nominally the new Lord of Jingzhou under his mother and uncles. Surrenders the region to Cao Cao without consulting Liu Bei; murdered on Cao Cao's order alongside his mother.
Liu Qi; bypassed for succession; flees to Jiangxia per Zhuge Liang's earlier counsel.
Kuai Yue; talks Liu Cong into surrendering — argues Cao Cao's army cannot be opposed.

Marches south with 500,000 troops; receives Jingzhou's surrender via Cai Mao's emissaries; orders the murder of Lady Cai and Liu Cong on the road; takes Cai Mao and Zhang Yun as his admirals. Pursues Liu Bei south.

Liu Bei; learns of Jingzhou's surrender too late; refuses to attack Liu Cong despite Zhuge Liang's strategic urging — 'I cannot bear to do it'; sets out south with the populace of Xinye and Fancheng following him voluntarily on Zhuge Liang's burn-Xinye plan.

Zhuge Liang; designs the second Xinye fire ambush — burns Cao Cao's vanguard with floods of pitch and oil arrows. Coordinates the southward exodus with the brothers across multiple guards. The chapter's headlining stratagem.
Guan Yu; commands part of the second Xinye ambush — releases the dammed river to drown Cao Ren's fleeing army.
Zhang Fei; commands part of the second Xinye ambush; harries Cao Cao's pursuit column.
Zhao Yun; commands part of the second Xinye ambush.
Liu Feng; commands part of the second Xinye ambush alongside Mi Fang.
Cao Hong; pursues Liu Bei south.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Ren; commands the pursuit of Liu Bei; his men drown when Guan Yu releases the dammed river.
Xiahou Dun; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Xiahou Yuan; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Li Dian; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Yu Jin; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Xu Chu; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Zhang He; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Zhang Liao; with Cao Cao in the southern campaign.
Zhang Yun; Cai Mao's partner — accepted as co-admiral of the Cao Cao fleet.
Zhang Yun (duplicate record).
Kong Rong; in this chapter publicly opposes Cao Cao's southern campaign; on Chi Lu's accusation Cao Cao has him executed with his whole family. The novel's farewell to Kong Rong.
Zhi Xi (Chi Lu); Cao Cao officer who informs on Kong Rong and engineers his execution.
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei on the southern exodus.
Mi Fang; commands part of the Xinye burn-and-flood ambush.
Mi Heng; named in passing in the chapter's recap of past Cao-court bloodlettings.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Yi Ji; defects with Liu Bei from Jingzhou.
Cai Yong; named in passing in the chapter's literary aside.
Wang Can; named in passing in the literary roll.
Song Zhong; envoy sent by Liu Cong to bring the surrender of Jingzhou to Cao Cao.
Deng Yi; minor figure named in passing.
Fu Xuan; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Fu; senior court official at Xuchang.
Xun Yu; running Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Sun Quan; named in passing — about to enter the Red Cliffs arc.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Yuan Shao; named in passing — long dead.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
